records.ety: [L. eradicatus, p. p. of eradicare to eradicate; e out + radix, radicis, root. See Radical.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Eradicated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Eradicating (?).]

defs: To pluck up by the roots; to root up; as, an oak tree eradicated.
To root out; to destroy utterly; to extirpate; as, to eradicate diseases, or errors.
quotes: This, although now an old an inveterate evil, might be eradicated by vigorous treatment.
